title: Decompose area with triangles
description: Find the areas of complex polygons by breaking them into triangles and rectangles.
topic_path: Module 5: Area, surface area, and volume problems
Topic A: Area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $2$ triangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part of the figure and the whole figure.**

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Triangle B | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Whole figure | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $2$ non-overlapping triangles.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of triangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle A} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 5\cdot 3\\\\
&=7.5
\end{align}$

The area of triangle A is $\redE{7.5}$ square units.

###Area of triangle B

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle B} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 10\cdot 3\\\\
&=15
\end{align}$

The area of triangle B is $\tealE{15}$ square units.

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{7.5}+\tealE{15}=\purpleD{22.5}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{22.5}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | $\redE{7.5}$
Triangle B | $\tealE{15}$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{22.5}$

options.alt: A quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides.  The base lengths are 5 units and 10 units.  The height is 3 units. A dashed line from opposite vertices creates two triangles, labeled A and B. Triangle A has a base of 5 units and a height of 3 units. Triangle B has a base of 10 units and a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $1$ triangle and $1$ rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part of the figure and the whole figure.**

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Rectangle B | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Whole figure | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $2$ non-overlapping shapes:  $1$ triangle and $1$ rectangle.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of triangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

The base of triangle A is $4$ units long because $9-5=4$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle A} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 4\cdot 6\\\\
&=12
\end{align}$

The area of triangle A is $\redE{12}$ square units.

###Area of rectangle B

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Rectangle B} &= \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=5\cdot 6\\\\
&=30
\end{align}$

The area of rectangle B is $\tealE{30}$ square units.

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{12}+\tealE{30}=\purpleD{42}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{42}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | $\redE{12}$
Rectangle B | $\tealE{30}$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{42}$

options.alt: A quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides.  The base lengths are 5 units and 9 units.  The height is 6 units. A dashed line that is perpendicular to the base creates two triangles, labeled A and B.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $3$ triangles and $1$ rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part of the figure and the whole figure.**

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Triangle B | $2$
Rectangle C | $4$
Triangle D | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Whole figure | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $4$ non-overlapping shapes:  $3$ triangles and $1$ rectangle.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of triangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

The base of triangle A is $10$ units long because $2+2+6=10$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle A} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 10\cdot 4\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

The area of triangle A is $\redE{20}$ square units.

###Area of triangle B

We're given that the area of triangle B is $\goldE{2}$ square units.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Area of rectangle C

We're given that the area of rectangle C is $\maroonE{4}$ square units.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Area of triangle D

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle D} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 6\cdot 2\\\\
&=6
\end{align}$

The area of triangle D is $\tealE{6}$ square units.

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{20}+\goldE{2}+\maroonE{4}+\tealE{6}=\purpleD{32}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{32}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | $\redE{20}$
Triangle B | $2$
Rectangle C | $4$
Triangle D | $\tealE{6}$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{32}$

options.alt: A 5 sided figure split into 4 sections by dashed lines.  Section A is a right triangle with a base of 10 units and a height of 4 units. Section B is a right triangle with a base of 2 units and a height of 2 units. Section C is a square with side lengths of 2 units. Section D is a right triangle with a base of 6 units and a height of 2 units.

hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $3$ non-overlapping shapes:  $2$ triangles and $1$ rectangle.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of triangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

The height of triangle A is $5$ units long because $9-4=5$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle A} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 6\cdot 5\\\\
&=15
\end{align}$

The area of triangle A is $\redE{15}$ square units.

###Area of rectangle B

We're given that the area of rectangle B is $\tealE{24}$ square units.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Area of triangle C

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle C} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 6\cdot 3\\\\
&=9
\end{align}$

The area of triangle C is $\maroonE{9}$ square units.

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{15}+\tealE{24}+\maroonD{9}=\purpleD{48}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{48}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Triangle A | $\redE{15}$
Rectangle B | $24$
Triangle C | $\maroonE{9}$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{48}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $1$ rectangle and $2$ triangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part of the figure and the whole figure.**

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Rectangle A | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Triangle B | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Triangle C | $5$
Whole figure | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $3$ non-overlapping shapes:  $1$ rectangle and $2$ triangles.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of rectangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

The height of rectangle A is $5$ because $10-5=5$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Rectangle A} &=\text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=8\cdot 5\\\\
&=40
\end{align}$

The area of rectangle A is $\redE{40}$ square units.

###Area of triangle B

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle B} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 6\cdot 5\\\\
&=15
\end{align}$

The area of triangle B is $\tealE{15}$ square units.

###Area of triangle C

We're given that the area of triangle C is $\maroonE{5}$ square units.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{40}+\tealE{15}+\maroonE{5}=\purpleD{60}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{60}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Rectangle A | $\redE{40}$
Triangle B | $\tealE{15}$
Triangle C | $5$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{60}$

options.alt: A 5 sided figure split into 3 sections by dashed lines.  Section A is a rectangle with a base of 8 units. Section B is a right triangle with a base of 6 units and a height of 5 units. Section C is a right triangle with a base of 2 units and a height of 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $1$ triangle and $2$ rectangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part of the figure and the whole figure.**

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Rectangle A | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
Triangle B | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Rectangle C | $21$
Whole figure | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
###Strategy

We have a figure split into $3$ non-overlapping shapes:  $1$ triangle and $2$ rectangles.

We can add the areas of each part to get the area of the whole figure.

###Area of rectangle A

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Rectangle A} &=\text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=6\cdot 9\\\\
&=54
\end{align}$

The area of rectangle A is $\redE{54}$ square units.

###Area of triangle B

[[☃ image 1]]

The base of triangle B is $3$ units long because $9-6=3$. The height of triangle B is $2$ units long because $9-7=2$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{Triangle B} &= \dfrac{1}{2}\cdot \text{base}\cdot \text{height}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot 3\cdot 2\\\\
&=3
\end{align}$

The area of triangle B is $\tealE{3}$ square units.

###Area of rectangle C

We're given that the area of rectangle C is $\maroonE{21}$ square units.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Area of whole figure

Now we can add all of the partial areas to get the whole area.

$\redE{54}+\tealE{3}+\maroonE{21}=\purpleD{78}$

The area of the whole figure is $\purpleD{78}$ square units.

Figure | Area (square units)
- | -
Rectangle A | $\redE{54}$
Triangle B | $\tealE{3}$
Rectangle C | $21$
Whole figure | $\purpleD{78}$

options.alt: A 5 sided figure made of a large rectangle with a small rectangle and small right triangle attached to the right side. A dashed line parallel to the height of the rectangle runs from the top, right vertex of the large rectangle to the bottom, right vertex of the large rectangle. This dashed line is the height of the right triangle and the height of the small rectangle that is attached to the right side of the large rectangle. The large rectangle, labeled A, has a base of 6 units and a height of 9 units. The right triangle is labeled B. The small rectangle, labeled C, has a height of 7 units. The base of the figure is 9 units.

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A 5 sided figure made of a rectangle with a triangle attached to the left. A dashed line parallel to the height of the rectangle runs from the top, left vertex of the rectangle to the bottom, left vertex of the rectangle. The dashed line is the base of the triangle that is attached to the left side of the rectangle. The triangle, labeled A, has a height of 2 units.
